claim: The ontology (internal/ontology) shall define no Observation or Evidence struct type anywhere -- Assumption remains the ontology's sole belief-carrying node type.
why: Wave 1 mechanical-honesty pass (2026-07-02): R-observation-evidence-scope (STRUCTURAL, M21 resolver verdict) makes a broader claim -- operator epistemics live in the working dialogue, crystallized only on request -- that is not fully machine-checkable (no static scan can verify 'lives in the dialogue'). But its NEGATIVE half -- no separate Observation/Evidence type exists -- IS mechanically checkable. Split out as its own atom (R-commit-boundary-checkable's slice-of-a-broader-claim pattern) so the mechanical slice can honestly reach ENFORCED while the parent claim (R-observation-evidence-scope) stays STRUCTURAL, undiluted by a partial enforcer. No Observation or Evidence struct is defined anywhere in internal/ontology/ -- Assumption (internal/ontology/assumption.go) is the sole belief-carrying node type. STRUCTURAL: the negative-existence guarantee is real (no such struct exists) but not machine-checked. (Historically the Python enforcement was an AST scan of spec/src/hotam_spec/*.py for a class named Observation or Evidence; an equivalent enforcer would scan internal/ontology/*.go for a struct of that name, but none is registered.) ENFORCED 2026-07-13: TestNoObservationType in internal/selfcheck/ontology_shape_test.go mechanically verifies this claim by static source scan: no type declaration named Observation or Evidence exists in non-test internal/ontology/*.go (Assumption remains the sole belief-carrying node type).
